title: Amperometric Estimation of Nickel and Cobalt with 1,2,3-Benzotriazole
author: S. N. PODDAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Inorganic Chemistry, Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science, Jadavpur, Calcutta-32 

  Manuscript received  19  January  1972;  revised  6  February  1973; accepted 26 May 1973 

 1,2,3-Benzotriazole has been employed as a reagent for the arnperometric titrations of nickel(II) and cobalt (II) in very dilute solutions. Nickel is titrated in presence of appropriate concentrations of ammonia and amonium acetate as the supporting electrolytes (pH 7.2) and cobalt in ammonia-ammonium chloride medium at about same pH. Titrations are not in any way hampered by the presence of different amounts of foreign metal ions like calcium, magnesium, alumi­nium, iron(III), chromium(III) and lead.
